name: The Sergeant's Gift
description: The drifting figure is deeper in the tunnels. You know who it is now. Go find it — find him.
accept_text: You return to Thorn with what Kell told you. He listens without moving. When you finish he is quiet for a long time. 'He was my sergeant for nine years,' he says finally. 'He went east with the patrol because I couldn't go myself. I sent him.' He doesn't look away. 'If the network has been directing him toward this — if he's been carrying something for months and waiting for the right person to give it to —' He stops. 'Go back. Find him. Whatever he has, he's had it for a long time. He's been waiting.'
complete_text: He doesn't look like what you expected. The crystals grow through his shoulders in symmetrical branches, catching the candlelight from Kell's stub of a candle. His eyes are clear. He watches you cross the chamber with something that might be recognition. He reaches into his uniform — the motion precise, unhurried — and removes a folded paper. He holds it out. You take it. Three hundred years old, the paper preserved by the crystal lattice in his hands. A patrol order addressed to a unit designation that doesn't exist in any current record. At the bottom, coordinates. He looks at you when you look up. He says one thing: 'They said someone would come.' Then he is still again.
